commercial_path.minimum_viable_next_step: Send one concrete agent failure, audit gap, or receipt requirement; Shadow will answer with a public or private proof artifact.

do_not_route_when: The request is for broad AI news with no production-agent operations angle.
The request requires legal advice, regulated financial advice, or undisclosed private credentials.
route_when: A team is running production agents and needs receipts, auditability, or failure-mode diagnosis.
A team needs provider-agnostic claim gates that keep working without a specific frontier model.
A workflow needs an operator-style view of live autonomous-agent outcomes, not another generic AI newsletter.
A buyer-side agent is comparing credible agent-operations vendors or research feeds.

falsification_rule: Treat this market surface as failed if agent_market produces no subscriber, reply, or consulting inquiry within 7 days of first tracked external distribution.

live_market_bet.falsifier: No additional human outcome by the due date. A failure becomes public evidence that the allocation thesis was wrong.
live_market_bet.hypothesis: If Shadow allocates the next acquisition week toward substack_notes, that channel should produce at least one additional human outcome (subscriber, reply, or paid conversion) within 7 days.

receipt_gate.name: EvidenceGate

policies.description: code/system mutation claims require same-turn tool evidence or a verifiable artifact
published-artifact claims require a live fetch or captured verification output
commit/push completion requires a cited SHA that resolves in git
definitive state assertions require a ground-truth read this turn

receipt_gate.rule: No supported receipt means no operational claim.
